service: Push notifications: RN (expo-notifications) -> Go GraphQL -> Expo Push API

implementation.mobile: src/services/notifications/* : requestPermission -> getExpoPushTokenAsync -> registerPushToken mutation; auto-register on login, unregister on logout; NotificationsScreen shows token + history
implementation.backend: push_tokens table (migration 0004); mutations registerPushToken/unregisterPushToken, query myPushTokens; sendTestPushNotification (self) and sendPushCampaign(userIds) -> push_sender.go calls Expo Push API with chunking + DeviceNotRegistered deactivation

errors: synthetic token rejected by Expo -> auto-deactivated
synthetic token rejected by Expo -> auto-deactivated

interpretation: The full path register -> list -> segment campaign (targeted by user id) -> test dispatch is exercised end-to-end; Go reaches the Expo Push API, validates the token, and deactivates invalid ones. A synthetic token is used because an Expo *web export* cannot mint a real device push token.
physicalBlocker: Real on-device delivery (mobile-10-push-received) requires a physical device or an Expo Go / EAS dev build to obtain a genuine ExponentPushToken. Not run here (no paid EAS build / no physical device); all code paths are implemented and proven up to the device boundary.
